title: Optimizing friction between a pad and a disc in an optical disc restoration device
abstract: The invention pertains in general to a latching mechanism for maintaining desired friction levels on an optical disc in an optical disc restoration device. In particular the invention pertains to devices, systems and methods for easily maintaining friction levels between pads and an optical disc in an optical disc restoration device for ease of adjusting friction settings during quality control, repair operation or when optimization settings are being set in an optical disc restoration device by a user.
claims:
Claims 1. An optical disc restoration device with a friction adjustment mechanism, including: a platen for holding an optical disc; at least one pad for polishing an optical disc; an adjustment screw, wherein rotation of said adjustment screw in one direction results in a larger distance between said pads and said optical disc during a restoration cycle and thereby lower friction between said pads and said optical disc during a restoration cycle and wherein rotation of said adjustment screw in another direction results in a closer distance between said pads and said optical disc and thereby higher friction between said pads and said optical disc during a restoration cycle. 2. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 1 further including a lock nut, wherein said lock nut holds said adjustment screw in a desired location. 3. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 2 further including a top lid, wherein said at least one pad is held in said top lid. 4. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 3 further including a hook, wherein said hook holds said top lid in a desirable position, thereby holding the pads to an optical disc at a desired friction setting. 5. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 2 further including an adjustable slide bracket, wherein said adjustable slide bracket slides said lock nut and said hook when said adjustment screw is rotated. 6. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 5 further comprising a return spring, wherein said return spring pulls said hook when said top lid is in an open position. 7. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 6 further including a spring bracket, wherein said spring bracket applies tension to said hook when said top lid is in an open position. 8. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 1 further comprising a water supply means. 9. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 8 further comprising an abrasive solution supply means. 10. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 2 further comprising one or more fan blades, wherein said fan blades reside in said top lid opposite said one or more pads. 11. The optical disc restoration device in accordance with claim 2 further comprising one or more exhaust ports in said top lid, wherein said exhaust ports removes vapor and heat from said optical disc restoration device. 12. A method for optimizing friction levels in an optical disc restoration device, the method comprising the steps of: providing an optical disc restoration device; and, providing an adjustable screw, wherein rotation of the adjustable screw in one direction results in higher friction between one or more pads and an optical disc during a restoration cycle and wherein rotation of said adjustable screw in another 

 direction results in lower friction between said one or more pads and said optical disc during a restoration cycle. 13. The method in accordance with claim 12 further comprising the step of providing an automated friction level notification when said adjustable screw is rotated. 14. The method in accordance with claim 13 further comprising the step of providing a hook for holding a friction level at a desired setting when said adjustable screw is being rotated. 15. The method in accordance with claim 14 further comprising the step of maintaining the friction level at a 3 ampere electric current. 16. A method for optimizing friction between one or more pads and an optical disc in an optical disc restoration device, the method comprising the steps of: providing a platen for holding an optical disc, wherein said platen is attached to a motor; providing one or more rotatable pads, wherein said pads are in contact with said optical disc during a restoration cycle; and, providing a friction adjustment mechanism, wherein rotation of said friction adjustment mechanism results in higher and lower friction levels between said rotatable pads and said optical disc during an optical disc restoration cycle. 17. The method in accordance with claim 16 further including the step of providing an adjustment screw as a friction adjustment mechanism, wherein rotation of said adjustment screw in one direction result in a higher friction setting and rotation of said screw in another direction results in a lower friction setting. 18. A method for optimizing friction between one or more pads and an optical disc in a optical disc restoration device, the method comprising the steps of: loading an optical disc onto a disc holder; initiating a disc restoration process; placing one or more pads in contact with said optical disc; rotating the adjustment screw in a direction to increase or decrease the friction of said one or more polishing pads on said optical disc; determining a correct pressure setting by optimizing current, pressure, and pad to disc distance; withdrawing one or more polishing pads from contact with said optical disc; and, removing a disc from said disc restoration device.
